notes.anchor: M. CICERO Q. PHILIPPO PROCOS. S.
notes.note: The letter-heading; the second of two letters to Q. Marcius Philippus, proconsul (PROCOS. = proconsule) in the East (Fam. 13.73-74). A renewed recommendation of the same two friends, L. Oppius and L. Egnatius.

notes.anchor: the business affairs of the absent L. Egnatius
notes.note: L. Egnatius Rufus was a Roman banker and knight with provincial business interests in the East; "absent" because he managed those affairs through agents while remaining at Rome. Cicero commends his interests as if they were his own.
